---
title: "Subsetting"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
# Load libraries and data
library(mia)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

data("GlobalPatterns", package = "mia")
tse <- GlobalPatterns

dim(tse)
#When subsetting by sample, expect the number of columns to decrease. When subsetting by feature, expect the number of rows to decrease.
```

```{r}
# Show the frequency of each value
tse$SampleType |>
    table() |>
    kable()
```

```{r}
# Subset by sample (column-wise)
tse_sub <- tse[, tse$SampleType %in% c("Feces", "Skin", "Tongue")]

# Show dimensions
dim(tse_sub)

```

```{r}
# Show the frequency of each value
rowData(tse)$Phylum |>
    table() |>
    head() |>
    kable()

#Subset by feature (row-wise)
# Subset by feature
#The first term with the %in% operator includes all the features of interest, whereas the second term after the AND operator & filters out all features that have an NA in place of the phylum variable.
selected <- rowData(tse)$Phylum %in% c("Actinobacteria", "Chlamydiae") &
    !is.na(rowData(tse)$Phylum)
tse_sub <- tse[selected, ]

# Show dimensions
dim(tse_sub)

```

```{r}
#Subset by sample and feature
# Subset by sample and feature and remove NAs
selected_rows <- rowData(tse)$Phylum %in% c("Actinobacteria", "Chlamydiae") &
    !is.na(rowData(tse)$Phylum)
selected_cols <- tse$SampleType %in% c("Feces", "Skin", "Tongue")
tse_sub <- tse[selected_rows, selected_cols]

# Show dimensions
dim(tse_sub)

```

```{r}
#Filtering based on library size
#As a preprocessing step, one might want to remove samples that do not exceed certain library size, i.e., total number of counts. Additionally, sometimes data might contain samples that do not contain any of the features present in the dataset. This can occur, for example, after data subsetting. To focus only samples containing sufficient information, we might want to remove those instances. In this example, we are interested only those features that belong to the species Achromatiumoxaliferum.

ind <- rowData(tse)$Species == "Achromatiumoxaliferum"
ind [is.na(ind)] <- FALSE
tse_sub <- tse[ind, ]

#Calculate the total number of counts in each sample
library(scuttle)

tse_sub <- addPerCellQCMetrics(tse_sub)
tse_sub$total |> head()

#Now we can see that certain samples do not include any bacteria. We can remove those.
# Remove samples that do not contain any bacteria
tse_sub <- tse_sub[,tse_sub$total !=0]
tse_sub

```

```{r}
#Filtering out zero-variance
#Calculate the standard deviation
rowData(tse)[["sd"]] <- rowSds(assay(tse, "counts"))

#Plot 
hist(log(rowData(tse)[["sd"]]))
```

```{r}
#From the histogram of feature variances, we can establish a sensible threshold for filtering. For example, using a threshold of 0 would effectively remove a large set of features that show no variance.

#It’s important to note that the data is on a log scale, meaning that a log-transformed value of 1 corresponds to 0 (i.e., log(1) = 0). This ensures that features with zero variance are correctly identified and filtered out.

th <- 1

selected <- rowData(tse)[["sd"]] > 1 
tse_sub <- tse[selected, ]
tse_sub
```

```{r}
#subset based on prevalence
tse_sub <- subsetByRare(tse, rank = "Genus", prevalence = 0.1, detection = 1)
tse_sub
```

```{r}
### Importing FILES ###

okeefe_assay <- read.csv("~/Downloads/okeefe_assay.csv", row.names = 1)
colnames(okeefe_assay) <- gsub("\\.", "-", colnames(okeefe_assay))

okeefe_coldata <- read.csv("~/Downloads/okeefe_coldata.csv", row.names = 1)
okeefe_rowdata <- read.csv("~/Downloads/okeefe_rowdata.csv", row.names = 1)


### Constructing TSE ###

tse <- TreeSummarizedExperiment(assays = list(counts = okeefe_assay),
                                colData = okeefe_coldata,
                                rowData = okeefe_rowdata)
dim(tse)

```

```{r}
library(scuttle)
tse <- addPerCellQCMetrics(tse)

colData(tse) |> head()

names (colData(tse))

library(miaViz)
plotHistogram(tse, col.var = "total")
```

```{r}
#Select samples that exceed specific library size threshold that you have selected based on histogram.

summary(colData(tse)$total)

th1 <- 3000
th2 <- 25000

tse_filtered <- tse [,colData(tse)$total > th1 & colData(tse)$total < th2]
tse_filtered

cat("Before:", ncol(tse), "samples\n")
cat("After:", ncol(tse_filtered), "samples\n")
#Subset data by selecting only prevalent features. Use counts assay. Specify detection threshold 1 and prevalence threshold 20%.

tse_prevalent <- subsetByPrevalent(
  tse_filtered, assay.type = "counts",
  detection = 1, 
  prevalence = 0.2)

cat("Before prevalence filtering:", nrow(tse_filtered), "features\n")
cat("After prevalence filtering:", nrow(tse_prevalent), "features\n")
```
